regulations.name: N-Cyclohexylbenzothiazole-2-Sulfenamide from the People's Republic of China: Initiation of Countervailing Duty Investigation
regulations.summary: N-Cyclohexylbenzothiazole-2-Sulfenamide (CBS) is a rubber vulcanization accelerator used in industrial rubber products, including components relevant to battery manufacturing and supply chains. The initiation of a countervailing duty (CVD) investigation by the U.S. Commerce Department signals potential new tariffs on this Chinese-origin chemical input, which may raise costs or disrupt sourcing for manufacturers relying on Chinese suppliers. For secondary battery domain concerns, this reinforces the need to evaluate Chinese supplier inclusion in SCM construction and assess feedstock supply chain risks tied to U.S. trade remedy actions.

regulations.name: Initiation of Antidumping and Countervailing Duty Administrative Reviews (April Anniversary Dates)
regulations.summary: Commerce's initiation of AD/CVD administrative reviews for April anniversary orders may directly affect battery material supply chains, particularly where Chinese suppliers of anode/cathode materials, lithium, or battery components are subject to existing duty orders. Companies building SCM frameworks must closely assess whether Chinese entities under review are included in their supplier base. Duty rate revisions resulting from these reviews could alter the landed cost of key battery raw materials and components, impacting procurement decisions and cost models for secondary battery R&D and production operations.

regulations.name: Antidumping or Countervailing Duty Order, Finding, or Suspended Investigation; Opportunity To Request Administrative Review and Join Annual Inquiry Service List
regulations.summary: This U.S. Commerce Department notice opens an administrative review window for existing antidumping (AD) and countervailing duty (CVD) orders, which are likely to include orders on Chinese battery materials (e.g., graphite, anode materials, lithium compounds) and related upstream inputs. For secondary_battery supply chain planning, the inclusion or exclusion of Chinese suppliers in the BOM and SCM must account for current AD/CVD duty rates, which can shift materially after each annual review. Any review outcomes affecting Chinese-origin battery raw materials will directly influence sourcing cost structures, origin declarations for battery passport compliance, and procurement risk assessments for recycled or primary feedstock.

regulations.name: 60-Day Notice of Proposed Information Collection: Application for Permanent/Temporary Export or Temporary Import of Classified Defense Articles and Classified Technical Data
regulations.summary: This notice proposes an information collection process for the export/import of Classified Defense Articles and Classified Technical Data, directly relevant to the space_environment domain's concern around export control eligibility for space-use components and materials. Organizations involved in space-related R&D that handle classified defense articles or technical data must anticipate updated compliance requirements when applying for export or temporary import permits. The 60-day public comment window (closing ~late July 2026) represents a narrow opportunity to influence final OMB-approved requirements before they become binding.

regulations.name: Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard No. 305a; Electric-Powered Vehicles: Electric Powertrain Integrity
regulations.summary: FMVSS No. 305a governs electric powertrain integrity for electric-powered vehicles, which directly implicates battery pack design, safety, and performance standards relevant to secondary battery R&D. Final rulemaking amendments may revise technical requirements affecting battery system integration, potentially influencing component certification, supply chain qualification, and BOM documentation needs. Organizations developing or supplying EV battery systems should monitor amended provisions for any changes to safety test protocols or material requirements that could affect product compliance timelines.

regulations.name: Commission Report on Progress Towards the Union-Wide CO2 Injection Capacity Target
regulations.summary: This Commission report monitors progress toward the EU's Union-wide CO2 injection capacity target, directly relevant to green_eco's feedstock supply chain and decarbonization infrastructure concerns. While it does not impose new compliance obligations, it signals the pace of Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS) infrastructure buildout in the EU, which could indirectly affect carbon-intensive manufacturing sectors — including battery and hydrogen supply chains — by shaping future carbon pricing and carbon removal obligations. The report reflects ongoing EU commitment to CCS as a climate tool, informing long-term Scope 3 and EPR strategic planning.

regulations.name: Support to Implementation of Critical Raw Materials Act (2024/1252) – Article 26 Circularity Guidance
regulations.summary: Article 26 of the Critical Raw Materials Act (2024/1252) mandates national measures on circularity, including a priority list of waste streams directly relevant to battery-grade critical raw materials (e.g., lithium, cobalt, nickel). This implementation support document shapes how member states will define collection and recycling obligations, directly affecting recycled content compliance requirements and supply chain sourcing decisions for battery manufacturers. R&D teams must prepare BOM-level traceability data and recycled-content certification pipelines in anticipation of binding national circularity measures derived from this framework.

regulations.name: Annual Report on R&TD Activities of the EU and Monitoring of Horizon Europe in 2025
regulations.summary: This annual EU Commission report monitors Horizon Europe R&D activities in 2025, which directly funds and shapes research trajectories relevant to next-generation battery technologies, raw material supply chains, and recycled content standards. It signals EU-level R&D prioritization that can influence battery passport data requirements, SCM decisions regarding non-EU (including Chinese) partners, and recycled material obligation frameworks. Teams should monitor funded project outcomes as they may translate into upcoming regulatory tightening on battery materials and processing.

regulations.name: 기후위기 대응을 위한 탄소중립·녹색성장 기본법 (탄소중립기본법)
regulations.summary: 한국의 탄소중립기본법은 green_eco 도메인 전반에 걸쳐 Scope 3 산정 의무화, 재활용 함량 기준 강화, EPR 분담금 체계 등 공급망 데이터 확보 및 순환경제 대응 요건을 법적 기반으로 뒷받침한다. secondary_battery 도메인에서는 배터리 원료 수급·재활용 원료 의무 함유량 관련 국내 이행 근거로 작용할 수 있으며, hydrogen 도메인의 청정수소 정의·인증 기준과도 연동될 가능성이 있다. 법 시행에 따른 하위 규정 제·개정 동향을 지속 모니터링하여 각 도메인별 의무 이행 타임라인을 조기에 파악할 필요가 있다.

regulations.name: 기후위기 대응을 위한 탄소중립·녹색성장 기본법 시행령
regulations.summary: 탄소중립기본법 시행령은 온실가스 감축, 녹색성장, 순환경제 관련 의무를 구체화하며, green_eco 도메인의 Scope 3 산정, 재활용 함량 의무, EPR 분담금 등 공급망 데이터 확보 및 환경 규제 이행에 직접적인 영향을 미친다. secondary_battery 도메인에서는 배터리 원료 공급망 및 재활용 원료 의무 함유량 관련 국내 이행 기준 설정의 근거가 될 수 있으며, 관련 기업의 BOM·원산지 데이터 준비 및 인증 체계 수립에 대응이 필요하다. hydrogen 도메인에서는 청정수소 정의 및 인증 기준, 보조금 적격성 판단의 상위 법적 근거로 작용하여 수소 사업 타임라인 및 재정 지원 요건에 영향을 줄 수 있다.

regulations.name: 우주개발 진흥법 (Space Development Promotion Act)
regulations.summary: 한국의 우주개발 진흥법은 우주 부품·소재의 국산화 및 민간 우주산업 생태계 육성을 법적으로 뒷받침하는 근거로, 우주 소부장 R&D 및 공급망 전략에 직접적인 영향을 미친다. 수출통제(전략물자) 적격성 판단 및 우주용 부품·소재 인증·시험 요건 변화와 맞물려 국내 우주 공급망 구축 시 준거 법령으로 작용한다. 민간 우주기업의 시장 진입 및 공급망 확보 전략 수립 시 본 법령의 개정 동향을 지속 모니터링할 필요가 있다.

regulations.name: 우주개발 진흥법 시행령
regulations.summary: 한국 우주개발 진흥법 시행령은 국내 우주개발 활동에 대한 법적 프레임워크를 규정하며, 우주용 부품·소재의 인증·시험 기준 및 수출통제 적격성에 직접적 영향을 미친다. 민간 우주기업의 공급망 구축 및 우주 소부장 국산화 정책과 연계되어 R&D 방향성 및 시장 진입 전략에 중요한 기준점을 제공한다. 안보 정책 변화에 따른 규제 변동 및 국가별 정책 편차와도 맞물려, 한국 내 우주 사업 수행 시 준수 의무 및 인허가 절차에 대한 지속적인 모니터링이 필요하다.

regulations.name: 우주개발 진흥법 시행규칙 (Enforcement Rules of the Space Development Promotion Act)
regulations.summary: 한국 우주개발 진흥법 시행규칙의 개정·시행은 우주용 부품·소재의 인증 및 시험 기준, 수출통제 관련 전략물자 관리 절차, 민간 우주기업의 허가·등록 요건 등에 직접적 영향을 미칠 수 있다. 국내 우주 소부장 국산화 사업 및 생태계 장려 정책의 법적 근거가 될 수 있어 R&D 방향 설정에 중요한 트리거로 작용한다. 또한 안보 정책 변화에 따른 규제 변동 리스크와 시장 진입 난이도 관련 국내 기준 변화를 면밀히 모니터링할 필요가 있다.

regulations.name: 수소경제 육성 및 수소 안전관리에 관한 법률 (수소법)
regulations.summary: 한국 수소법은 청정수소의 정의·인증 기준과 수소경제 육성을 위한 지원 체계를 규율하며, 수소 관련 사업자의 보조금 적격성 및 인증 요건에 직접적인 영향을 미친다. 청정수소 인증 기준의 변동은 프로젝트 타임라인 및 사업성 평가에 리스크 요인으로 작용할 수 있다. 또한 수소 안전관리 규정은 수소 설비·운송·저장 관련 규제 준수 부담을 수반하며, 공급망 구축 시 국내 인증 요건 대응이 필요하다.

regulations.name: Regulation for Federal Financial Assistance (OMB Proposed Revision)
regulations.summary: OMB's proposed revision to Federal Financial Assistance guidance introduces stricter transparency, accountability, and oversight requirements for federal grants and cooperative agreements, which directly affects hydrogen R&D projects (e.g., IRA 45V-linked programs) reliant on federal award funding. Tightened oversight rules may impose additional reporting and compliance burdens on subsidy recipients, potentially affecting grant eligibility timelines and increasing administrative overhead for projects already under time pressure from the 2028 IRA 45V construction deadline. Indirect effects may also extend to secondary battery and green/eco R&D programs that rely on federal cooperative agreements or grants for supply chain or recycled content initiatives.

regulations.citation_quote: OMB is proposing revisions that would improve transparency, accountability, and oversight for Federal awards across the Federal Government. This includes ensuring that American tax dollars are not wasted or misused

regulations.name: Further Adjusting the Tariff Regimes for Imports of Aluminum, Steel, and Copper Into the United States
regulations.summary: Tariff adjustments on aluminum, steel, and copper imports directly affect the cost and availability of key raw materials used in battery cell manufacturing, enclosures, and current collectors (e.g., copper foil for anodes). Supply chain strategies involving Chinese suppliers face heightened exposure. Green hydrogen and space hardware sectors relying on these structural metals for electrolyzers, pressure vessels, and satellite components will also see input cost increases.

regulations.name: Significant New Use Rules on Certain Chemical Substances (25-2.5e)
regulations.summary: EPA's SNURs require 90-day advance notification before manufacturing or processing designated chemical substances under TSCA, directly affecting battery material suppliers and importers who may use novel chemical substances (e.g., electrolytes, electrode materials, additives) subject to PMN orders. This adds regulatory lead time and compliance overhead to the supply chain qualification process, particularly for new chemistries in next-generation battery development. Companies must audit their bill of materials to identify any SNUR-listed substances before commencing new manufacturing or import activities.

regulations.citation_quote: The SNURs require persons to notify EPA at least 90 days before commencing the manufacture (defined by statute to include import) or processing of any of these chemical substances for an activity that is designated as a significant new use in the SNUR.

regulations.name: Potassium Phosphate Salts From China; Institution of Five-Year Reviews
regulations.summary: Potassium phosphate salts (e.g., KH₂PO₄, K₃PO₄) are key precursor materials used in lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery cathode manufacturing. The institution of five-year sunset reviews on antidumping/countervailing duty orders against Chinese suppliers raises the risk of continued or increased trade barriers on these inputs, directly affecting raw material sourcing strategies and SCM decisions regarding Chinese supplier inclusion. Battery passport and BOM preparation must account for potential origin-related compliance complications if these duties are revoked or extended.

regulations.citation_quote: The Commission hereby gives notice that it has instituted reviews pursuant to the Tariff Act of 1930, as amended, to determine whether revocation of the countervailing and the antidumping duty orders on potassium phosphate salts from China would be likely to lead to continuation or recurrence of material injury.

regulations.name: Melamine From China; Institution of Five-Year Reviews
regulations.summary: The five-year review of antidumping and countervailing duty orders on melamine from China signals continued U.S. scrutiny of Chinese chemical supply chains, which is indirectly relevant to secondary battery and green/eco domains that rely on Chinese feedstock sourcing. If duties are upheld or expanded, supply chain mapping (SCM) decisions regarding Chinese suppliers — including melamine-adjacent chemical intermediates — may be affected. This also reinforces the broader trend of U.S. trade measures targeting Chinese materials, which battery and green material teams should factor into BOM and origin-tracking preparations for Battery Passport and Scope 3 reporting.

regulations.name: In the Matter of Hans De Geetere and Knokke-Heist Support Corporation Management (Hasa Invest) – BIS Final Decision and Order
regulations.summary: This Final Decision and Order from the Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) concerns an export control enforcement action against individuals and entities in Belgium, indicating active US export control enforcement against foreign parties. This is directly relevant to the space_environment domain's concern around export control eligibility, as BIS actions of this type can restrict access to controlled US-origin goods, technology, and software — including space-grade components and materials. R&D teams sourcing US space-qualified parts or collaborating with international partners must monitor BIS denied/restricted party lists to ensure compliance and avoid supply chain disruptions.

regulations.name: 국가연구개발혁신법 (National R&D Innovation Act)
regulations.summary: 한국의 국가연구개발혁신법은 국가 R&D 사업의 기획·관리·성과 활용 전반을 규율하며, 이차전지·수소·우주·녹색기술 등 전략 분야의 R&D 투자 방향과 연구기관의 규정 준수 의무에 직접적인 영향을 미친다. 동법은 연구개발 결과물의 소재·부품 원산지 및 기술이전 관련 관리 체계를 포함할 수 있어, SCM 구축 및 배터리 소재 데이터 준비와 연계된 컴플라이언스 리스크가 존재한다. 우주·수소·이차전지 분야 국산화 R&D 사업 선정 및 보조금 적격성 판단에도 본 법의 절차적 요건이 적용된다.

regulations.name: 산업기술연구조합 육성법
regulations.summary: 본 법은 산업기술연구조합의 설립·운영을 지원하는 한국 국내법으로, 이차전지·수소·우주·친환경 등 R&D 컨소시엄 구성 및 공동연구 활동의 법적 근거를 제공한다. 연구조합을 통한 공동 R&D 수행 시 소재·부품·장비 국산화 및 기술개발 협력 생태계 구축에 간접적으로 기여하며, 이차전지 차세대 소재 및 우주 소부장 국산화 사업 관련 R&D 트리거로 활용 가능하다. 다만, 직접적인 수출통제·인증·환경규제 등 특정 규제 의무를 부과하는 법령은 아니므로, 감시 도메인에 대한 직접적 규제 영향은 제한적이다.

regulations.name: REACH Regulation Article 117(3) Sixth Report – Alternatives to Animal Testing (June 2026)
regulations.summary: This report documents the EU's progress on replacing animal testing under REACH, signalling a tightening of chemical substance data requirements via alternative test methods. For green_eco domains, this indirectly affects feedstock and material supply chains where chemical safety data packages (used for DPP and Scope 3 reporting) must be compiled using validated non-animal methods, potentially delaying or complicating substance dossier submissions. Secondary_battery stakeholders sourcing novel electrode materials or electrolyte chemicals under REACH may face evolving data requirements as accepted alternative test protocols are updated, impacting material qualification timelines.

regulations.name: Rescission of Climate-Related Disclosure Rules
regulations.summary: The proposed rescission of SEC climate-related disclosure rules would eliminate mandatory Scope 3 emissions and climate data reporting obligations for US-listed registrants, directly reducing the pressure on green_eco domain actors to prepare DPP and Scope 3 supply chain data. However, since this is still a proposal, compliance frameworks already under development should not be dismantled prematurely. For secondary_battery and hydrogen domains, this may ease near-term reporting burdens related to feedstock supply chain disclosures and clean hydrogen certification data in US capital markets contexts, but EU-side obligations (e.g., CSRD, Battery Passport) remain unaffected.

regulations.citation_quote: The Securities and Exchange Commission ("Commission") proposes to rescind amendments to its rules under the Securities Act of 1933 ("Securities Act") and Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ("Exchange Act") that require registrants to provide certain climate- related information in their registration statements and annual reports.

regulations.name: Notice of Determinations and Request for Comments Concerning Actions in Section 301 Investigations — Forced Labor Import Prohibition
regulations.summary: The USTR's determination that 54 economies have failed to enforce forced labor import prohibitions directly threatens battery supply chains reliant on upstream materials (e.g., lithium, cobalt, graphite) sourced from flagged economies, most notably China. Companies must urgently audit whether Chinese or other implicated suppliers appear in their BOM and origin data, as Section 301 trade actions could restrict or tariff imports of battery raw materials and components. This intersects with Battery Passport requirements around origin and recycled content traceability, and with SCM decisions on whether to include Chinese firms — heightening the need for pre-prepared, item-level supply chain data.

regulations.name: Request for Comments on the Scope and Operation of a Mechanism To Promote Reciprocal Managed Trade With China
regulations.summary: USTR의 미·중 쌍방향 관리무역 메커니즘(U.S.-China Board of Trade) 수립 논의는 배터리 원료(리튬·흑연 등) 공급망 내 중국 기업 포함 여부 및 관세 조정 범위에 직접적 영향을 미친다. '비민감 품목'에 대한 호혜적 관세 완화 협상이 현실화될 경우, 중국산 배터리 소재·부품의 수입 조건 변화와 SCM 구성 전략 재검토가 필요하다. 또한 중국 수출통제 품목(전극 소재·차세대 배터리 장비 등)이 협상 대상에서 제외될 가능성이 있어 장비 도입 리스크 및 원료 수급 리스크에 대한 지속 모니터링이 요구된다.

regulations.name: Certain New Chemicals; Receipt and Status Information for November 2025, December 2025, and January 2026 (TSCA)
regulations.summary: TSCA new chemical review covers Premanufacture Notices (PMNs) and Significant New Use Notices (SNUNs) for substances that may include battery-relevant chemicals (e.g., electrolyte materials, electrode precursors, or novel lithium compounds). Any new chemical receiving an EPA restriction or consent order under TSCA could affect feedstock sourcing and supply chain decisions for secondary battery materials. Biotechnology exemption applications and test marketing exemptions (TMEs) noted in this filing may also apply to next-generation battery chemistries under development, warranting monitoring for compliance obligations tied to new material introductions in the US market.

regulations.name: 군수품관리법 시행령
regulations.summary: 군수품관리법 시행령은 군수품의 조달·관리·수출통제 체계를 규율하며, space_environment 도메인에서 우주용 부품·소재의 전략물자 해당 여부 및 수출통제 적격성(ITAR/EAR 유사 국내 통제)에 직접적인 영향을 미칠 수 있다. 군수품으로 분류되는 우주 소부장 품목은 조달·공급망 구성 시 국내 규제 준수 의무가 발생하며, 민간 우주기업의 공급망 참여 및 국산화 사업 추진 시 해당 시행령상 요건을 사전 검토해야 한다. 안보 정책 연계 규제 변동 가능성이 있어 지속적인 모니터링이 필요하다.

regulations.name: Certain Corrosion Inhibitors From the People's Republic of China: Preliminary Results of the Antidumping Duty Administrative Review; 2024-2025
regulations.summary: This antidumping duty review targets Chinese producers/exporters of corrosion inhibitors, confirming sales below normal value during March 2024–February 2025. Corrosion inhibitors are used in battery manufacturing processes and electrolyte formulations, meaning potential duty imposition could raise input costs and complicate Chinese supplier inclusion in SCM builds. This adds to the broader supply chain risk landscape for secondary battery materials sourced from China, reinforcing the need to evaluate Chinese entity exposure ahead of final duty determinations.

regulations.citation_quote: Commerce preliminarily determines that producers/exporters subject to this review made sales of subject merchandise at less than normal value (NV) during the period of review (POR) March 1, 2024, through February 28, 2025.

regulations.name: Foreign-Trade Zone (FTZ) 52 – Proposed Production Activity; Photonics Industries International, Inc. (Laser Systems); Ronkonkoma, New York
regulations.summary: This FTZ notification covers proposed laser system manufacturing activity by Photonics Industries International in Ronkonkoma, NY, which is directly relevant to space_environment concerns around export control eligibility (ITAR/EAR/전략물자). Laser systems produced within an FTZ may benefit from duty deferrals or exemptions on foreign-origin inputs, potentially affecting the supply chain structure and component sourcing decisions for space-grade laser/photonics products. The FTZ status of such production facilities can intersect with export control compliance obligations, as FTZ-produced goods incorporating controlled foreign components may face additional scrutiny under ITAR/EAR before export.

regulations.name: Deep Seabed Mining: Notice of Receipt of Application for Deep Seabed Mining Exploration Licenses and Announcement of Public Comment Period and Virtual Public Hearing
regulations.summary: NOAA의 심해저 광물 채굴 탐사 라이선스 신청 접수는 망간단괴·코발트리치 크러스트 등 배터리 핵심 원료(코발트, 니켈, 망간 등)의 새로운 공급망 형성 가능성을 시사합니다. 중국 의존도가 높은 배터리 원료 수급 다변화 관점에서 모니터링이 필요하며, 향후 채굴 허가 시 원산지·공급망 구성(중국 기업 포함 여부 등) 판단에 직접적인 영향을 줄 수 있습니다. Battery Passport의 원산지·재활용원료 항목 대응 시 미국산 심해저 광물의 적격성 여부도 선제적으로 검토할 필요가 있습니다.

regulations.name: Agency Information Collection Activities: Clearance of a Reinstatement Approval of Information Collection: Part 60 – Flight Simulation Device Initial and Continuing Qualification and Use
regulations.summary: This FAA notice under the Paperwork Reduction Act seeks OMB approval to reinstate information collection requirements under 14 CFR Part 60, governing flight simulation device qualification and use. While primarily targeting civil aviation training, the regulation is tangentially relevant to the space_environment domain as FAA oversees both commercial aviation and commercial space launch licensing. The reinstatement of Part 60 compliance burdens—covering training, testing, checking, and experience documentation—may signal broader FAA regulatory activity in simulation and certification frameworks that could intersect with commercial space crew qualification requirements. Direct operational impact on secondary_battery, green_eco, or hydrogen domains is negligible.

regulations.name: European Competitiveness Fund (ECF) Regulation, including Defence Research and Innovation Programme (COM(2025)0555)
regulations.summary: The ECF establishes a new EU-level funding mechanism specifically including a programme for defence research and innovation, which directly affects space_environment domain actors through potential R&D funding eligibility, supply chain security incentives, and shifting EU industrial/security policy priorities. For secondary_battery and hydrogen domains, the ECF may indirectly open competitive funding streams for clean technology and advanced materials R&D aligned with EU strategic autonomy goals. The defence R&D programme component may also tighten export control alignment and dual-use considerations for space components and materials, increasing regulatory complexity for market entry and supply chain sourcing.

regulations.name: 군수품관리법 (Military Supplies Management Act)
regulations.summary: 군수품관리법은 군수품의 조달·관리·처분에 관한 법적 체계를 규정하며, 우주·방산 부품 및 소재의 수출통제 적격성(전략물자 해당 여부)과 직결될 수 있습니다. 방산 및 우주용 소재·부품의 국산화, 공급망 관리, 수출입 통제 의무 등에 간접적 영향을 미칠 수 있으며, 우주 소부장 국산화 사업 추진 시 군수품 해당 여부 판단이 필요합니다. 한국 내 우주·방산 공급망 구축 및 민간 우주기업의 부품 조달 전략 수립 시 본 법의 적용 범위를 사전 검토해야 합니다.

regulations.name: N-Cyclohexylbenzothiazole-2-Sulfenamide From the People's Republic of China: Initiation of Less-Than-Fair-Value Investigation
regulations.summary: The U.S. Commerce Department's initiation of a Less-Than-Fair-Value (anti-dumping) investigation into N-Cyclohexylbenzothiazole-2-Sulfenamide (CBS), a rubber vulcanization accelerator, signals tightening U.S. trade actions against Chinese chemical inputs. While CBS is primarily a rubber additive, it is used in components relevant to battery packaging and sealing materials; any resulting anti-dumping duties could increase costs and disrupt supply chains for manufacturers sourcing this chemical from China. This is directly relevant to secondary_battery domain concerns around Chinese supply chain inclusion decisions (SCM 구축 시 중국 기업 포함 여부 판단) and raw material supply regulation (원료 수급 규제), reinforcing the need to audit Chinese-origin chemical inputs in the BOM ahead of potential duty imposition.

regulations.name: Building for the Future Through Electric Regional Transmission Planning and Cost Allocation (FERC)
regulations.summary: This FERC rulemaking on electric regional transmission planning and cost allocation directly shapes the infrastructure backbone for grid-connected renewable energy and green economy projects in the US. Cost allocation reforms can affect the economics of large-scale clean energy integration, indirectly influencing feedstock and supply chain decisions for green/eco R&D operations. A compliance extension motion signals regulatory timeline uncertainty that project planners must monitor.

regulations.name: 원자력 진흥법
regulations.summary: 원자력 진흥법은 원자력 기술 및 산업 전반의 진흥을 규율하며, 우주용 방사성 동위원소 전원(RTG 등) 및 원자력 추진 관련 부품·소재의 수출통제 및 전략물자 해당 여부에 간접적 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다. 국내 우주 소부장 국산화 및 R&D 생태계와 연계될 경우 원자력 기반 우주 기술 개발의 규제 환경을 형성합니다. 다만 현재 제공된 본문 내용이 제목 수준에 그쳐 구체적 조항 확인이 필요합니다.
